AnatomicSegment.description:
GI segments relevant to IBD endoscopic assessment.
Includes upper GI (CD), lower GI (CD + UC), and post-surgical anatomy.
AnatomicSegment.title: AnatomicSegment

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text (false by default).
has_mention.title: Has Mention

ContinuousPattern.description: Pattern of inflammation across the examined extent.

continuous.description: Whether inflammation is described as continuous. TRUE: means continuous/uninterrupted involvement is documented. FALSE: means inflammation is described as discontinuous, patchy, skip, or segmental. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: means continuity is not mentioned or cannot be determined.

ContinuousPatternMention.title: ContinuousPatternMention

max_segment_reached.description: Most proximal segment reached, e.g. terminal_ileum.

skip_lesions_present.description: True if discontinuous involvement is documented.
skip_lesions_present.title: Skip Lesions Present

rectal_involvement.description: True if rectum is inflamed, False if rectal sparing is documented, None if rectum not assessed. Drives UC vs CD discrimination downstream.
rectal_involvement.title: Rectal Involvement

upper_gi_involvement.description: True if any upper GI segment shows IBD-attributable findings.
upper_gi_involvement.title: Upper Gi Involvement

ileal_involvement.description: True if TI or neo-TI shows IBD-attributable findings.
ileal_involvement.title: Ileal Involvement

MucosalAppearance.description:
Elemental mucosal descriptors. Multiple may apply to a single segment.
Intentionally fine-grained so downstream code can compose any score.
MucosalAppearance.title: MucosalAppearance

MucosalAppearanceMention.description:
A single mucosal appearance descriptor mentioned for a bowel segment.

Use multiple MucosalAppearanceMention objects when more than one descriptor
applies to the same segment.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text (false by default).
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

MucosalAppearanceMention.title: MucosalAppearanceMention

PhysicianGlobalAssessmentMention.description:
Physician global assessment (PGA) of overall endoscopic disease severity.

Chart review instruction:
Capture the endoscopist's overall qualitative impression of inflammatory
severity for the bowel segment or exam being described. Prefer explicit
summary statements such as "normal", "minimal disease", "mild colitis",
"moderate inflammation", or "severe active disease".

Do not calculate or infer a score.
Do not upcode based only on individual findings such as erythema,
friability, erosions, or ulcers unless the report also gives an overall
severity impression.

Use:
- NONE: explicitly no active inflammatory disease / normal / remission
- MINIMAL: explicitly minimal disease or minimal activity
- MILD: explicitly mild disease/activity/inflammation/colitis
- MODERATE: explicitly moderate disease/activity/inflammation/colitis
- SEVERE: explicitly severe disease/activity/inflammation/colitis
- NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: no clear overall physician global assessment stated

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text (false by default).
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

physician_global_assessment.description: Physician overall free-text global assessment of endoscopic severity. Record only an explicitly stated overall severity impression, not a derived score. NONE: no active inflammatory disease or normal/remission; MINIMAL: minimal disease/activity; MILD: mild disease/activity; MODERATE: moderate disease/activity; SEVERE: severe disease/activity; NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: no explicit overall physician global assessment is documented.

PhysicianGlobalAssessmentMention.title: PhysicianGlobalAssessmentMention

SegmentFindings.description:
All endoscopic findings for a single anatomic segment.
One SegmentFinding per segment examined (or explicitly negated).

examined.description: True if the segment was actually visualized during the procedure; False otherwise.
examined.title: Examined

severity_qualifier.description: Severity documented for the findings in this segment.

appearances.description: Mucosal appearances documented in this segment
appearances.title: Appearances

ulceration.description: Ulcer findings documented in this segment

stenosis.description: Stenosis character findings documented in this segment

fistula_orifice.description: Fistula orifice findings documented in this segment

continuous.description: Distribution pattern documented in this segment

SegmentFindings.title: SegmentFindings

SeverityQualifier.description:
Free-text severity descriptor as documented by the endoscopist.
NOT a score. Downstream endpoint code may translate, but this field
only captures what the report says.
SeverityQualifier.title: SeverityQualifier

surface_area_percent.description: Approximate percent of segment surface area ulcerated, if reported.
surface_area_percent.title: Surface Area Percent

description:
Document-level extraction of endoscopic findings from a single colonoscopy
or sigmoidoscopy report. Disease-agnostic at the extraction layer; Mayo
endoscopic subscore and SES-CD derivation are computed downstream.

Variables:
* procedure: Procedure metadata including type, extent of examination, and preparation quality.
* continuous: Whether mucosal involvement follows a continuous (UC-typical) or skip-lesion pattern.
* segment_findings: Per-segment endoscopic findings (e.g., erythema, ulceration, stenosis) for each examined anatomical segment.
* endoscopist_global_assessment: Endoscopist's overall impression of disease activity or mucosal appearance.
title: IbdEndoscopyAnnotation
